name: Medical-RAG – Private Healthcare AI
raw_facts: Architecture: Local-first Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system designed for HIPAA-compliant, private medical data processing.
LLM Architecture: Utilizes `BioMistral-7B` (Quantized GGUF) via `ctransformers`, optimized for medical domain reasoning on consumer-grade CPUs with AVX2 support.
Embedding Strategy: Implemented `NeuML/pubmedbert-base-embeddings`, a specialized model trained on PubMed abstracts, to ensure high semantic fidelity for clinical terminology.
Vector Storage: Integrated `Qdrant` as the primary vector database, supporting real-time similarity search and scalable collection management.
Ingestion Pipeline: Built a recursive PDF processing engine using `langchain.document_loaders.PyPDFLoader` with `RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter` (1000/100 chunking strategy).
API Layer: Developed a non-blocking FastAPI backend with `Jinja2` templating for low-latency query-response cycles.
Data Privacy: The system is designed to run entirely air-gapped, leveraging local LLMs and vector stores to ensure 100% data residency.
Inference Optimization: Configured `ctransformers` with dynamic thread allocation (half of available OS cores) to balance throughput and system stability.
business_impact: Enabled secure clinical decision support by providing citation-backed answers from private oncology handbooks and research papers.
Reduced infrastructure costs by $500+/mo compared to enterprise LLM APIs by shifting inference to local hardware.
